hypothesis: Replacing fixed diversity thresholds in NaviDiv with an online Bayesian optimization of the similarity-, fragment-, and n-gram-penalty coefficients to enforce a target mean pairwise Tanimoto similarity of 0.12 will, over 1000 REINVENT4 steps for singlet fission, yield at least a 15% higher final average property score than the fixed combined-constraints regime while keeping the achieved similarity within ±0.01 of the target.
